response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack engineer with genuine GIS/geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and has demonstrated distributed systems thinking at Sociomantic. However, the role demands modern, commercial-grade C++ or Rust as a primary skill — his C++ use is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, with no evidence of continued or production-level use. His primary stack for the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not transfer directly to low-level routing tile pipeline work. There is no experience with routing data, navigation guidance, tile generation, or traffic integration, which are the core deliverables of this position.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications, demonstrating familiarity with geo-data modeling and collection pipelines.
Experience building low-latency, high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second), showing comfort with performance-sensitive distributed systems.
Consistent application of quality practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles, which aligns with the role's rigorous QA expectations.
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (2014–2015 at NI, one thesis project) — no evidence of modern C++ (C++17/20) in production commercial environments or open-source projects.
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume, eliminating the alternative path to meeting the core language requirement.
No routing, navigation guidance, or tile pipeline experience; GIS work is focused on ecological data collection rather than optimized routing or real-time traffic integration.
